name: event-intel-mcp
description: Turn exhibitor lists (URL / HTML / CSV / text) into evidence-backed BD target tier lists. 8 MCP tools, local mini-RAG (bge-m3 + Chroma).
long_description: event-intel-mcp scores exhibitor companies against a product context (capability cards) using a 7-dimension scoring model with an evidence-floor lifecycle (has_url + has_news ceiling). Two flows: (1) product context lifecycle — draft, validate, ingest capability cards into a local Chroma collection; (2) event pipeline — URL/HTML/CSV → source acquisition → extraction → enrichment (Brave web/news) → fit retrieval (event evidence → product collection) → scoring → tier_list.md + tier_list.yaml. Supports Anthropic Claude or ChatGPT Plus OAuth as LLM backends.

tools.name: check_runtime
tools.description: Verify bge-m3 cache / Chroma writable / API keys / product context for a workspace.

tools.name: draft_capability_cards
tools.description: Draft a capability_cards.yaml from source docs (md/txt/pdf) or inline text.

tools.name: validate_capability_cards
tools.description: Validate a hand-edited capability_cards.yaml against schema v1 (pydantic SSOT).

tools.name: ingest_product_context
tools.description: Embed validated capability cards via bge-m3 and upsert into Chroma product_{workspace_id} collection.

tools.name: build_event_tier_list
tools.description: Score exhibitors from an HTML/CSV/text source against the workspace's product context. Writes tier_list.md + tier_list.yaml.

tools.name: analyze_event_page
tools.description: Classify an exhibition site URL (static_html | xhr_endpoint | embedded_json | operator_capture_required | login_required) and return acquisition hints.

tools.name: probe_exhibitor_endpoint
tools.description: Probe XHR / embedded-JSON candidate endpoints surfaced by the analyzer and return the winning data source.

tools.name: acquire_exhibitor_source
tools.description: End-to-end URL acquisition: analyze → probe → fetch → write artifact + sha256 manifest. Returns (source_kind, source_ref) ready for build_event_tier_list.

python_path.title: Python interpreter path
python_path.description: Path to the Python interpreter where event-intel-mcp deps are installed (must have run `pip install -e .` against the repo). The default below assumes the conda env from the setup docs — confirm or adjust it.

brave_api_key.title: Brave Search API key
brave_api_key.description: Used by build_event_tier_list enrichment (web + news search). Optional here — leave blank to supply it via BRAVE_API_KEY in the repo's .env instead.

use_chatgpt_oauth.title: Use ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription instead of an Anthropic API key
use_chatgpt_oauth.description: Check this to drive the LLM with your ChatGPT subscription (OAuth) instead of an Anthropic API key. After installing, run `event-intel login-chatgpt` once in a terminal to authenticate. Leave unchecked to use the Anthropic API key below.

anthropic_api_key.title: Anthropic API key
anthropic_api_key.description: For the default Anthropic path. Leave blank if you checked 'Use ChatGPT' above, or to supply it via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the repo's .env instead.

warm_on_start.title: Preload the embedding model when Claude Desktop starts
warm_on_start.description: Check to load the bge-m3 model (~1.3 GB) in the background as soon as the server starts, so your first tier-list build is fast. Adds ~1.3 GB resident memory for the session and only runs if the model is already downloaded (`event-intel models prepare`). Leave unchecked to load it on first use instead.
